evals.name: standard-review-feedback
evals.prompt: I got review comments on PR #142 in lfx-v2-ui. Can you address them?
evals.expected_output: Agent fetches PR threads, categorizes comments, presents a plan, makes changes, commits with summary, responds to threads, resolves them, and posts a summary comment.

assertions.description: Agent presents a structured plan categorizing all comments before making any changes
Commit message includes PR #142 and @-mentions the reviewers
Commit body itemizes each file change with reviewer attribution
Commit message includes Signed-off-by line
Each review thread gets an individual response
Thread responses reference the commit SHA
Uses resolveReviewThread mutation for addressed threads
Posts a single summary comment on the PR
Summary organizes items by type

evals.name: mixed-feedback
evals.prompt: My PR for the committee member bio field has review feedback — some code changes and a question about why I used a signal instead of BehaviorSubject. Address what you can and flag what needs my input.
evals.expected_output: Agent categorizes comments into code changes vs questions vs discussion, presents plan with needs-your-input items, pauses for approval, handles each category differently.

assertions.description: Distinguishes code changes, questions, and discussion items
bio_updated_at discussion flagged as needing user input
Agent waits for user approval before making changes
Signal vs BehaviorSubject question gets explanation, not code change
Commit does not claim discussion items were resolved
Does NOT resolve threads needing user direction

evals.name: auto-detect-branch
evals.prompt: Fix the review comments on my current PR
evals.expected_output: Agent auto-detects no PR on main, asks user, then addresses only unresolved threads.

assertions.description: Recognizes main branch has no PR and asks the user
Thread 3 (resolved) excluded from plan
Only threads 1 and 2 appear in the plan
Commit references PR #205 and @jorge
Each unresolved thread gets individual response
Posts summary comment on PR
